title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Authorization, Ethics, Privacy, and Evidence · Guided Program Studio
summary: A public-interest site-quality team incrementally builds authorized stakeholder and evidence charter from original fixed response and HTML fixtures while retaining earlier authorization, HTTP, URL, provenance, politeness, accessibility, testing, security, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of authorized stakeholder and evidence charter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Define the stakeholder decision, users, page population, evidence, success, failure, review cadence, and observable value before choosing crawler technology.
Record who controls the target, what access is authorized, which identities and paths are permitted, and who can pause or revoke the crawl.
Separate technical policy from terms, contracts, copyright, privacy, and jurisdiction questions and route uncertain cases to qualified review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stakeholder Outcome
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop site-quality team handles case 2859 during a clean package and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the crawl charter on authorization, stakeholder value, minimized data, and honest evidence limits; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stakeholder Outcome.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define the stakeholder decision, users, page population, evidence, success, failure, review cadence, and observable value before choosing crawler technology. For Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stakeholder Outcome, keep Stakeholder Outcome connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Evidence Boundaries
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-evidence-boundaries-xybdc6" that uses case 1241 to gate the crawl charter on authorization, stakeholder value, minimized data, and honest evidence limits. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Evidence Boundaries.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Evidence Boundaries into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Evidence Boundaries, keep Evidence Boundaries connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-evidence-boundaries-xybdc6
# Competency: scraper-evidence-boundaries.
# Case 1241: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_evidence_boundaries_xybdc6(authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 8, minimized_fields: int = 6, retained_fields: int = 6, transfer_gates_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_xybdc6 = "xybdc6-6cdbyx-1241"
    assert evidence_variant_xybdc6
    evidence_axes_xybdc6 = (
        "authorizationxybdc6",
        "url1241",
        "response6cdbyx",
        "treexybdc61241",
        "frontier1241xybdc6",
        "report6cdbyx1241",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_xybdc6)) == 6
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "authorization-missing"
    if acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "outcome-underdefined"
    if minimized_fields < 1 or retained_fields != minimized_fields:
        return False, retained_fields, "data-minimization"
    if not transfer_gates_named:
        return False, retained_fields, "evidence-overclaim"
    assert authorized and acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "authorized-crawl-charter"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
